description: Document and codify the grouped automatic-invoices model: parent rows group by `client + invoice window`, child rows remain the atomic execution units.
Refactor the automatic-invoices ready-work view model so each group carries explicit parent summary data separate from child execution rows.
Add stable parent-group identity and grouped selection keys for parent rows, distinct from child selector inputs.
Replace the flat automatic-invoices ready table with an expandable parent/child grouped UI.
Render parent-row summaries including client name, invoice window, child count, aggregate amount, and combinability state.
Render child-row detail with contract or assignment identity, cadence source, billing timing, service period, and amount.
Compute parent combinability from effective invoice-level financial scope: client, currency, PO scope, tax source, and export shape.
Enable the parent checkbox only when the currently eligible children are combinable as one invoice.
Support child-level selection regardless of parent combinability, including tri-state parent behavior for partial selection.
Implement smart `Select All` semantics that select parents for combinable groups and child rows individually for non-combinable groups.
Keep blocked child candidates visible inside groups while preventing their selection and reflecting their status in the parent summary.
Expose explicit incompatibility reasons on non-combinable parent rows, including PO, currency, tax, and export-shape differences.
Add grouped preview request semantics so the preview stack can accept parent selections and exact child selections rather than only one selector input.
Render preview output that clearly states whether the current selection will produce one invoice or several, with grouped or split preview presentation as appropriate.
Add grouped generation request semantics so parent selections and arbitrary child selections execute exactly the selected scope.
Implement exact-scope grouped execution so selected parent or child rows never re-expand into unselected sibling candidates.
Support true combined invoice generation for compatible grouped selections spanning multiple child candidates.
Preserve fan-out generation for incompatible grouped selections so the same grouped UI can still produce multiple invoices safely.
Redesign duplicate-prevention and idempotency logic so grouped parent selections and multi-child executions are safe against re-invoicing.
Redesign invoice persistence so a combined multi-assignment invoice does not require a fake single `client_contract_id` invoice owner.
Make charge-level assignment attribution authoritative for combined invoices and preserve that attribution through billing persistence.
Update invoice reads, history, and billing query surfaces so combined invoices remain explainable and queryable without relying on invoice-header assignment ownership alone.
Keep purchase-order enforcement safe by disabling combined execution when selected children do not share compatible PO scope and by preserving correct PO behavior for valid combinations.
Preserve existing single-assignment and single-child recurring invoice behavior so the grouped model is additive rather than regressive.
Update billing docs, runbooks, and in-product explanatory copy to describe grouped selection, combinability, and smart `Select All` semantics.
